plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-593 RP19.P04.M06.S06-RP19.P05.M04.S07 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-593 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP19 p04 closeout p05 foundation: the P04 Synthetic Fixture Set tail (the fifteen remaining rows), the full P04 Test And Golden Case Set, Hermes Evidence Packet, Claude Review Packet, and Closeout And Next Handoff cycles, the full P05 Scope Inventory, Contract Draft, Type And Shape Definition, and Primary Implementation Slice cycles, and the P05 Secondary Workflow Slice head (the first seven rows) as ten descriptor-only sections over 150 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H19), Claude review packet (C19), and CP00-594 handoff, without opening client portal, secure link, client review, or watermark runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-593 is the 11th pack in the RP19 client-portal descriptor-only series (CP583-CP593) and is internally consistent across every reviewed artifact. Identity/scope (pack_id CP00-593, risk C, 150 units, range RP19.P04.M06.S06-RP19.P05.M04.S07, upstream CP00-592, next CP00-594/RP19.P05.M04.S08) matches the prompt baseline, review-baseline.json, closeout-pack-plan.json, next-pack-queue.json (live_latest CP00-592, live_next RP19.P04.M06.S06), the registry pack binding, and the embedded contract descriptor. Implementation in registry.js/service.js/validators.js follows the established descriptor-only pattern: freezeCp593Result asserts every no-write/no-real-data flag false (no client portal/secure link/watermark/review runtime, no state writes, no DB rows, no object storage, no Hermes runtime receipt, no command runtime, no real fixture data), and the descriptor validator enforces descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, and raw_payload_included=false on all 150 rows. Distribution reconciles to 150 (phase RP19.P04=85 / RP19.P05=65; deliverables claude_review14/ui35/security_audit12/implementation37/fixture36/test10/hermes_evidence6; ten micro sections). Coverage/slice validators, model.test.js, and the contract validation script use the real plan pack and deepEqual the contract against registry constants, so contract drift is gated; command-evidence.json reports all 27 build/validate/test commands at exit 0. Authority boundary is intact: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval=false, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, and Hermes appears only as a descriptor_only no_write receipt shape (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ review hardening holds: the readiness auth-preflight passed cleanly (exit 0, 1456 stdout bytes, well-formed success JSON, not fenced/tool-call-shaped/malformed), and forbidden_review_evidence enumerates exactly the required rejection set (failed auth, zero-byte, malformed/fenced/tool-shaped JSON, disabled tools, missing raw-output/source-inspection basis, Claude-final-approval, source-mutation, enterprise-trust claims). The pack-level production_ready claim is honestly reported as false (status implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); production_ready_candidate=true is a local descriptor-verification flag only and does not overclaim or weaken gates. The only surface anomaly (the pack's own untracked docs listed in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved) was checked against CP592's identical, successfully-closed scope file and confirmed to be by-design tooling behavior with active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; final human approval still governs any enterprise-trust or runtime-opening claim.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-593 client portal core p04 closeout p05 foundation descriptor pack
